Input.description: A detailed log of all inputs and clicks recorded during the trial.

duration.description: Describes for how long the mouse button was pressed, in seconds.
duration.title: Duration

input_id.description: Primary key; each input or click has its own identifier value that is unique within the table.
input_id.title: Input Id

is_object_enabled.description: Indicates whether the object that was clicked on was enabled (clickable) or not.
is_object_enabled.title: Is Object Enabled

object_name.description: The human-readable name of the object that was clicked on (e.g., "sos_box_1_3").
object_name.title: Object Name

object_state.description: Describes the state the object was in *before* it was clicked on. The meaning of "state" depends on the particular task (e.g., "new empty").
object_state.title: Object State

object_type.description: Describes the type of object that was clicked on (e.g., "button").
object_type.title: Object Type

onset.description: Duration between the start of the trial and the moment the mouse button press occurred, in seconds.
onset.title: Onset

option_id.description: If the click is on an option, this variable indicates which option it was.
option_id.title: Option Id

response_element_index.description: Indicates which of the clicks is used and in what order to form the actual response in the response table when `response_structure` is "sequence" or "set".
response_element_index.title: Response Element Index

response_id.description: Refers to the `response_id` in the `Response` table and indicates for which response this input was recorded.
response_id.title: Response Id

stimulus_id.description: If the click is on a stimulus, this variable indicates which stimulus it was.
stimulus_id.title: Stimulus Id

x_screen.description: X coordinates of the click relative to the left edge of the screen in pixels.
x_screen.title: X Screen

x_viewport.description: X coordinates of the click relative to the left edge of the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen width.
x_viewport.title: X Viewport

y_screen.description: Y coordinates of the click relative to the top edge of the screen in pixels.
y_screen.title: Y Screen

y_viewport.description: Y coordinates of the click relative to the top edge of the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen height.
y_viewport.title: Y Viewport

Input.title: Input

Instrument.description: Describes the instrument used for data acquisition.

block_id.description: Specific parameterization of the instrument for a single block of trials (e.g., "DS_FORWARD_PRACTICE" and "DS_FORWARD_TEST"). Block-level parameters override timeline-level parameters.
block_id.title: Block Id

description.description: A human readable, compact description of the main aspects of the instrument.
description.title: Description

instrument_id.description: Unique identifier of an instrument. It can be formed by combining name and version (e.g., "DS_v2024.01").
instrument_id.title: Instrument Id

link.description: External link, if any, the provides more information about the instrument, e.g., on Cognitive Atlas.
link.title: Link

name.description: Name of the toolkit (scene, code, or configuration) that is used to collect the data, e.g., "DS" for a software that runs digit span task in forward OR backward order. The specific parameterization of the instrument is defined by the "Timeline" (e.g., a variant of instrument called "DS_FORWARD").
name.title: Name

timeline_id.description: Identifier for a timeline. Timeline is a variant of an instrument with specific parametrization, for the whole experiment or for multiple block of trials (e.g., "DS_FORWARD" and "DS_BACKWARD" for digit span task). Timelines are configurations that specify instrument parameters.
timeline_id.title: Timeline Id

version.description: Refers to the specific version/build of a particular instrument. We will use a calendar based versioning system ([calver.org](https://calver.org/); e.g., "v2024.01").
version.title: Version

Instrument.title: Instrument

Option.description: Describes each option that a subject could choose from in a trial.

animation.description: Describes the animation used to display a specific option in a human-readable format. For example, "fadeIn 3s ease-in-out" indicates a 3-second fade-in animation.
animation.title: Animation

description.description: A human readable, compact description of the main aspects of the option. The description for a given option depends on the task but follows a specific template for a given task. Because of this, it looks like the option description could be parsed---however, this is not the intention; structured, parsed data will be available in other fields; description is for human readability and facilitates the understanding of the data.
description.title: Description

duration.description: How long the option was displayed or active in seconds, starting from the `onset`, in seconds.
duration.title: Duration

index_in_source.description: When a option is picked from a particular set (e.g., `digits1to9`), this index refers to the index within that set.
index_in_source.title: Index In Source

index_in_trial.description: Indexing each of the options within the set of options that were available to subjects on a given trial.
index_in_trial.title: Index In Trial

object_id.description: An option is defined by a set of features. This variable is used to identify each time the same features were used. For example, if the same white digit "3" is used as an option in the Digit Span task, all those instances would have the same `object_id` although they would have different `id`s (as they appeared at different times).
object_id.title: Object Id

onset.description: Duration between the start of the trial and the moment the option was displayed or activated, in seconds.
onset.title: Onset

option_id.description: Primary key of the `Option` table. References to this identifier in other tables are thus named `option_id`.
option_id.title: Option Id

panel_id.description: Identifier of the pane this option was displayed over.
panel_id.title: Panel Id

response_id.description: Refers to the `response_id` in the `Response` table and indicates for which response this option was shown.
response_id.title: Response Id

source.description: Refers to the specific generator or set from which the option originates. Options sharing the same source have the same data structure and can be thus collectively described in a table named after `option_source` (e.g., "digit1to9").
source.title: Source

source_type.description: An option is typically created using a particular procedure/algorithm or is sampled from a particular set. This variable indicates which of these two applies for the current option.
source_type.title: Source Type

trial_index.description: Refers to the `trial_index` in the `Response` table and indicates in which trial this option was shown.
trial_index.title: Trial Index

value.description: A numeric value associated with a particular response option; typically indicating the "worth" of a response (e.g., `value=1` for the correct response).
value.title: Value

x_screen.description: X coordinates of the option component on the screen in pixels.
x_screen.title: X Screen

x_viewport.description: X coordinates of the option component on the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen width.
x_viewport.title: X Viewport

y_screen.description: Y coordinates of the option component on the screen in pixels.
y_screen.title: Y Screen

y_viewport.description: Y coordinates of the option component on the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen height.
y_viewport.title: Y Viewport

Option.title: Option

OptionComponent.description: Options can comprise multiple components. This table describes each component of an option.

color_hex.description: The hexadecimal RGB color code of the component (e.g., #FF0000 for red) with optional alpha channel for transparency.
color_hex.title: Color Hex

color_name.description: The human-readable name of the component color, e.g., `red`.
color_name.title: Color Name

description.description: A human readable, compact description of the component.
description.title: Description

index.description: A 1-based index differentiating each part and indicating the stacking order of them. An option component with a higher `index` is displayed on top of those with lower values, similar to CSS z-index property.
index.title: Index

option_id.description: A reference to the key `option_id` in the `Option` table.
option_id.title: Option Id

panel_id.description: Identifier of the panel this option is displayed over.
panel_id.title: Panel Id

symbol_count.description: The number of symbols represented in this component.
symbol_count.title: Symbol Count

symbol_layout.description: How the symbols are laid out.
symbol_layout.title: Symbol Layout

symbol_name.description: The name of the displayed symbol.
symbol_name.title: Symbol Name

x_screen.description: X coordinates of the option component relative to the left edge of the screen in pixels.
x_screen.title: X Screen

x_viewport.description: X coordinates of the option component relative to the left edge of the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen width.
x_viewport.title: X Viewport

y_screen.description: Y coordinates of the option component relative to the top edge of the screen in pixels.
y_screen.title: Y Screen

y_viewport.description: Y coordinates of the option component relative to the top edge of the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen height.
y_viewport.title: Y Viewport

OptionComponent.title: OptionComponent

Response.description: Main table where each row describes a response in a trial.

accuracy.description: A task-dependent accuracy measure ranging from 0 to 1 (inclusive).
accuracy.title: Accuracy

activity_index.description: When subjects complete multiple activities (e.g., a cognitive test followed by a questionnaire), this variable indicates the order of each activity (i.e., the first activity completed by the subject has `activity_index` = 1, the second activity has `activity_index` = 2; even if the second activity is an exact repetition of the first one).
activity_index.title: Activity Index

adaptive_method_config.description: More detailed configuration for the adaptive method, including initial values, step sizes, and termination criteria.
adaptive_method_config.title: Adaptive Method Config

adaptive_method_name.description: Specifies the adaptive procedure used to modify instrument parameters in response to subject performance (e.g., `staircase`).
adaptive_method_name.title: Adaptive Method Name

adaptive_parameter_name.description: Specific instrument parameter that is dynamically modified based on the subject's performance.
adaptive_parameter_name.title: Adaptive Parameter Name

adaptive_parameter_value.description: The specific value of the instrument parameter that was used for this trial. This value is updated as the adaptive algorithm adjusts the parameter based on the subject's responses.
adaptive_parameter_value.title: Adaptive Parameter Value

adaptive_parameter_value_next.description: The next value of the adaptive parameter that will be used in the subsequent trial, as determined by the adaptive algorithm.
adaptive_parameter_value_next.title: Adaptive Parameter Value Next

additional_measures.description: Indicates whether additional measures have been recorded during this trial and if so what kind of measures they are. Possible values include (non-exhaustive):
additional_measures.title: Additional Measures

agent_id.description: A unique identifier assigned to the agent (typically person) generating the responses. This ID tracks their participation and responses throughout the study. See `Agent` table.
agent_id.title: Agent Id

block_index.description: Refers to the order in which this block has been experienced by the subject. When there are multiple blocks, this variable indicates the order of each block (i.e., the first block completed by the subject has `block_index=1`, the second block has `block_index=2`, even if the second block is an exact repetition of the first one).
block_index.title: Block Index

block_name.description: The name of a particular block in a timeline. If the same block is completed twice in a row, they would have different `block_index` values (1 and 2, respectively) but they would have the same `block_name` (e.g., “NB_timeline1_block1”). More details about the `block_name` is available in the `Instrument` table.
block_name.title: Block Name

block_type.description: Specifies the experimental role of the block (e.g., tutorial, practice, test, instruction).
block_type.title: Block Type

correct.description: Indicates whether the response matches the expected response (i.e., correct = TRUE) or not (i.e., correct = FALSE).
correct.title: Correct

episode_index.description: Episodes are temporally distinct bins of time (no overlap and discrete). The binning of the time into successive episodes depends on the task; it is mostly used and necessary to group data in cases where two distinct trials occurred at the same time (e.g., dual N-back).
episode_index.title: Episode Index

evaluation_label.description: There are several labels that can be assigned to a given response to specify what that response means in terms of evaluation within a task. The most general terms are "correct” and "error" (which are already given by the correct variable). There are however more specific sets of terms that may apply in different contexts. For example, in a signal detection task, it is common to use labels from the signal detection theory framework (i.e., “hit”, “miss”, “false alarm”, “correct rejection”). In other contexts, researchers might use terms like “omission” or “commission” errors or even things like “perseveration” error (e.g., in the Wisconsin Card Sorting Test). Note that these terms are not always well defined or exclusive. For example, a “hit” is also a “correct” response and a “false alarm” may be synonymous to “commission error”. Whenever possible use the more specific terms (i.e., always use “hit” rather than “correct” when applicable). Here are few evaluation labels that are commonly used:
evaluation_label.title: Evaluation Label

expected_response_description.description: A description of the expected response using the same convention as response_description.
expected_response_description.title: Expected Response Description

expected_response_option_index.description: The index of the option the subject is expected to choose from the set of options.
expected_response_option_index.title: Expected Response Option Index

feedback_description.description: Lists the different kinds of feedback that were shown on a given trial. When multiple types of feedback were used, feedback will list them using `;` as a separator. If a given type of feedback was shown multiple times during a trial, that feedback type is listed only once (i.e., feedback_description does NOT represent the sequence of feedbacks). The possible values for feedback are:
feedback_description.title: Feedback Description

group_name.description: Subjects may be assigned to different groups. Typically, different groups will have different experiences within a study.
group_name.title: Group Name

input_action_type.description: Refers to the type of action the subject performs to give a response. Possible values include (non-exhaustive):
input_action_type.title: Input Action Type

input_count.description: The number of inputs (i.e., actions) the user made during the trial.
input_count.title: Input Count

input_interface_type.description: Refers to the type of interface subjects used to input actions. Possible values include (non-exhaustive):
input_interface_type.title: Input Interface Type

instrument_id.description: The unique identifier of the instrument used for collecting data (e.g., the name of the computer script used to run the test). Unique in the `Instrument` table and corresponding files in the `instruments/` folder.
instrument_id.title: Instrument Id

instrument_repetition.description: The number of times this particular instrument has already been completed anytime in the past by this particular subject in this study. This variable has a value 0 the first time an instrument is used.
instrument_repetition.title: Instrument Repetition

job_description.description: The more specific description of a job, which gives more information about what the participant sees and has to do. Whereas the `job_type` typically uses only verbs and adjectives, the `job_description` also contains nouns (e.g., “recall-digits-forward”, “recall-letters-backward”).
job_description.title: Job Description

job_repeat.description: Whether this trial's job has not been seen before in this timeline (i.e., specific version of the instrument).
job_repeat.title: Job Repeat

job_type.description: The general type of operation the subject needs to perform. The job typically is expressed as a verb (e.g., "recall", "sort") and can be the same for different instruments (e.g., Digit Span test and Spatial Span test both have a job of type "recall-forward").
job_type.title: Job Type

language.description: The language the task was completed in, expressed as a two-letter code within the [ISO_639-1 standard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes).
language.title: Language

measurement_type.description: Describes the type of measurement implied by Option which in turn has implications on how that data should be processed during analysis; takes a value in:
measurement_type.title: Measurement Type

multitask_type.description: Subjects may be required to perform multiple tasks at the same time. This variable indicates the type of multitasking required.
multitask_type.title: Multitask Type

option_count.description: The number of options the participant can choose from on a given trial.
option_count.title: Option Count

option_data_type.description: Describes the type of data this option entails. Possible values include:
option_data_type.title: Option Data Type

option_id.description: Is a unique identifier for the option (set or generator) used on a given trial.
option_id.title: Option Id

option_source.description: Refers to the specific generator or set that determined the options on a given trial. Option that stem from the same source have the same data scheme and could thus be described in a table named after option_source (i.e., option_source indicates which table contains the full information about the option set).
option_source.title: Option Source

option_source_type.description: A set of options is typically created using a particular procedure/algorithm (“generator”) or is sampled from a particular set (“set”). This variable indicates which of these two applies for the current options.
option_source_type.title: Option Source Type

outcome_description.description: Describes the observable consequences of the subject's response (e.g., "the opened box is empty").
outcome_description.title: Outcome Description

outcome_numeric.description: A numeric value describing the observable consequences of the subject's response (e.g., +3 points).
outcome_numeric.title: Outcome Numeric

response_count.description: Each trial contains by definition only one response. However, when response_structure is other than unitary, a response comprises multiple pieces of information (e.g., “3-5-7” could be one response in the digit span task and this response contains three components, namely “3”, “5” and “7”). response_count refers to the number of components that make up a response (not the number of responses within a trial).
response_count.title: Response Count

response_datetime.description: The datetime corresponding to the completion of the response.
response_datetime.title: Response Datetime

response_description.description: A description of participant’s response; typically the description of the option that was chosen.
response_description.title: Response Description

response_id.description: A unique identifier assigned to responses in temporal order, meaning that larger IDs correspond to more recent responses that occurred later in time. This ID is unique within this table; no two rows share the same value.
response_id.title: Response Id

response_initiation_time.description: In some cases (e.g., a reaching movement) it might be useful to encode when a response was initiated.
response_initiation_time.title: Response Initiation Time

response_numeric.description: A numeric value associated with a particular response; this could be a numeric value entered directly by the subject or the numeric meaning of a selected option (for example, the choice of option "Never" may be associated with the numeric value of 0). Note that this variable describes the subject's response; it does not describe the value (e.g., correctness or goodness) that is associated with that response.
response_numeric.title: Response Numeric

response_option_index.description: The index of the option the participant chose, starting from 1.
response_option_index.title: Response Option Index

response_skipped.description: In some cases (e.g., in some questionnaires), subjects have the option to skip a question.
response_skipped.title: Response Skipped

response_structure.description: The structure of the response required by the subject; can take values in:
response_structure.title: Response Structure

response_time.description: The duration, in seconds, between the earliest possible time a response could have been completed and the moment that response was actually completed (and NOT when it was initiated).
response_time.title: Response Time

response_validation_time.description: In some cases, subjects may need to press an extra key to validate previous responses. When relevant, this variable may encode this duration.
response_validation_time.title: Response Validation Time

score.description: A numeric value associated with a particular response in a given context. This variable may be used to compute a performance metric or a questionnaire level index (e.g., a well-being score).
score.title: Score

session_index.description: When there are multiple sessions, this variable indicates the order of each session (i.e., the first session completed by the subject has `session_index` = 1, the second session has `session_index` = 2; even if the second session is an exact repetition of the first one).
session_index.title: Session Index

session_uuid.description: A globally unique identifier (UUID) for the session, assigned by the recording engine when the session starts. Unlike `session_index` (a per-subject ordinal), `session_uuid` identifies the session across datasets and systems; it is the value event streams refer to when scoping events to a session.
session_uuid.title: Session Uuid

stimulus_count.description: The number of stimuli shown to the participant during the trial.
stimulus_count.title: Stimulus Count

stimulus_description.description: A human readable, compact description of the main aspects of the stimulus. The description for a given stimulus depends on the task but follows a specific template for a given task. Because of this, it looks like the stimulus_description could be "parsed" and "tidied"---however, this is not the intention; parsed/tidied data will be available in other tables; description is for human readability and facilitates the understanding of the data.
stimulus_description.title: Stimulus Description

stimulus_id.description: Is a unique identifier for the (unitary, set or sequence of) stimuli presented during a trial; if those exact same stimuli are repeated in a different trial, that trial would have the same value for stimulus_id. stimulus_id may also be used to refer to a specific message or question in a questionnaire.
stimulus_id.title: Stimulus Id

stimulus_index.description: Indexes in chronological (or spatial) order the stimuli shown within an instrument (counting one stimulus per response). `stimulus_index` may for instance be used to refer to the nth question asked within a questionnaire.
stimulus_index.title: Stimulus Index

stimulus_index_in_source.description: Index of the stimulus within the table referred to by stimulus_source. : For example, if stimulus_source == "digit1to9", stimulus_index_in_source = 1 refers to "1" while for stimulus_source == "LettersAtoD", stimulus_index_in_source = 1 refers to "A".
stimulus_index_in_source.title: Stimulus Index In Source

stimulus_onset.description: Duration between the start of the trial and the appearance of the stimulus, in seconds.
stimulus_onset.title: Stimulus Onset

stimulus_panel_count.description: The number of panels or screen areas stimuli may appear on during the trial. For example, in a task where stimuli to be compared are presented on the left and right side of the screen, stimulus_panel_count = 2.
stimulus_panel_count.title: Stimulus Panel Count

stimulus_position_index.description: Refers to discrete positions on the screen the stimulus may appear on. The set and ordering of possible positions depends on the test. Whenever possible, it follows a natural order (left to right, top to bottom), but in free-form layouts, indices are arbitrary.
stimulus_position_index.title: Stimulus Position Index

stimulus_role.description: A stimulus may play different roles within a trial. Below is a list of some possible roles:
stimulus_role.title: Stimulus Role

stimulus_set_size.description: The number of different values each presented stimulus could have taken. This value gives an indication of the complexity of the stimulus space. When this number is large we set this variable to infinity, when for any reason it was not computed, it has a value of NA.
stimulus_set_size.title: Stimulus Set Size

stimulus_source.description: Refers to the specific generator or set the stimulus belongs to. Stimuli that stem from the same source have the same data scheme and could thus be described in a table named after stimulus_source (i.e., stimulus_source indicates which table contains the full information about the stimulus; e.g., "digit1to9").
stimulus_source.title: Stimulus Source

stimulus_source_type.description: A stimulus is typically created using a particular procedure/algorithm ("generator") or is sampled from a particular set ("set"). This variable indicates which of these two applies for the current stimulus.
stimulus_source_type.title: Stimulus Source Type

stimulus_structure.description: We distinguish three stimulus structures: unitary, set, sequence
stimulus_structure.title: Stimulus Structure

stimulus_structure_source.description: Refers to the specific generator used to produce the stimulus_structure (e.g., sequence of digits in a digit span test). When no generator was used, this variable has a value of none.
stimulus_structure_source.title: Stimulus Structure Source

stimulus_structure_source_type.description: Indicates the type of method used to generate the stimulus_structure (this is relevant when a trial displays a sequence of or set of stimuli): none, preset, generator
stimulus_structure_source_type.title: Stimulus Structure Source Type

stimulus_type.description: BDM distinguishes the following stimulus types: messages and questions
stimulus_type.title: Stimulus Type

study_name.description: The name of the study or experiment.
study_name.title: Study Name

task_index.description: when `multitask_type` is not empty, `task_index` refers to each of the individual tasks. For example, for auditory-visual dual N-back, `task_index=1` is the auditory task and `task_index=2` is the visual task.
task_index.title: Task Index

timed_out.description: Some tasks require subjects to give a response within a certain time limit. When subjects fail to respond before that time runs out, timed_out is set to TRUE.
timed_out.title: Timed Out

timeline_id.description: Timelines are specific parameterization of an instrument and their identifiers are unique within the corresponding table for the instrument in the `instruments/` folder.
timeline_id.title: Timeline Id

timeline_repetition.description: The number of times this particular timeline has already been completed anytime in the past by this particular subject in this study. This variable has a value 0 the first time a timeline is completed.
timeline_repetition.title: Timeline Repetition

transformation_name.description: Refers to the specific events-to-trials function used to construct rows of this table from raw events. The transformation (or projection in DDD terminology) embodies the definition of a trial for a particular task. The transformer name refers to a code in the format of a function `f(trial_state, event) => trial_state`, where `event` is the event occurred during the performance of the task, and `trial_state` is the data stored for the trial. The final state of a trial is thus the result of applying a sequence of projections such that `trial = f(f(f(initial()), e), e), e)`.'
transformation_name.title: Transformation Name

trial_index.description: Sequential identifier representing number of times transformation rule to the events occurred. It increases with each re-computation of the trial based on updated or newly received events.
trial_index.title: Trial Index

trial_seed.description: Random seed used in the trial (if any).
trial_seed.title: Trial Seed

trial_start_datetime.description: The datetime at which the first event of the trial occurred.
trial_start_datetime.title: Trial Start Datetime

Response.title: Response

Stimulus.description: Describes each of the stimuli that were shown during a trial.

animation.description: Describes the animation used to display a specific stimulus in a human-readable format. For example, "fadeIn 3s" indicates a 3-second fade-in animation.
animation.title: Animation

description.description: A human readable, compact description of the main aspects of the stimulus. The description for a given stimulus depends on the task but follows a specific template for a given task. Because of this, it looks like the `description` could be parsed and converted into a more structured format---however, this is not the intention; structured data will be available in other tables; here, description is for human readability and facilitates the understanding of the data.
description.title: Description

duration.description: Describes for how long this stimulus was displayed after its onset, in seconds.
duration.title: Duration

index_in_source.description: When a stimulus is picked from a particular set (e.g., "digits1to9"), this index refers to the index within that set.
index_in_source.title: Index In Source

index_in_trial.description: Refers to individual stimuli within the sequence or set of stimuli shown during a trial.
index_in_trial.title: Index In Trial

object_id.description: A stimulus is defined by a set of features. This variable is used to identify each time the same stimulus features were used.
object_id.title: Object Id

onset.description: Duration between the start of the trial and the appearance of the stimulus, in seconds.
onset.title: Onset

panel_id.description: Identifier of the panel this stimulus is displayed over.
panel_id.title: Panel Id

presentation_id.description: In a multitasking setting, a particular instance of a stimulus (e.g., the current letter "A") may be used by multiple tasks at the same time (e.g, in the dual N-back task). Because these are different trials, they will have different `trial_index` values and hence will have different rows in the `Stimulus` table. We use `presentation_id` to indicate that a given stimulus is in fact the same instance across those trials.
presentation_id.title: Presentation Id

response_id.description: Refers to the `response_id` in the `Response` table and indicates for which response this stimulus was shown.
response_id.title: Response Id

role.description: Describe the role that the stimulus plays in the trial, e.g., "target".
role.title: Role

source.description: Refers to the specific generator or set the stimulus belongs to.
source.title: Source

source_type.description: A stimulus is typically created using a particular procedure/algorithm ("generator") or is sampled from a particular set ("set"). This variable indicates which of these two applies for the current stimulus.
source_type.title: Source Type

stimulus_id.description: Primary key of the `Stimulus` table. References to this identifier in other tables are thus named `stimulus_id`.
stimulus_id.title: Stimulus Id

trial_index.description: Refers to the `trial_index` in the `Response` table and indicates in which trial this stimulus was shown.
trial_index.title: Trial Index

x_screen.description: X coordinates of the stimulus on the screen in pixels.
x_screen.title: X Screen

x_viewport.description: X coordinates of the stimulus on the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen width.
x_viewport.title: X Viewport

y_screen.description: Y coordinates of the stimulus on the screen in pixels.
y_screen.title: Y Screen

y_viewport.description: Y coordinates of the stimulus on the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen height.
y_viewport.title: Y Viewport

Stimulus.title: Stimulus

StimulusComponent.description: Stimuli can comprise multiple components. This table describes each component of a stimulus.

color_hex.description: The hexadecimal RGB color code of the component (e.g., #FF0000 for red) with optional alpha channel for transparency.
color_hex.title: Color Hex

color_name.description: The human-readable name of the component color, e.g., `red`.
color_name.title: Color Name

description.description: A human readable, compact description of the component.
description.title: Description

index.description: A 1-based index indicating the stacking order of stimulus components. A stimulus component with a higher `index` is displayed on top of those with lower values, similar to CSS z-index property.
index.title: Index

stimulus_id.description: A reference to the primary key `id` of the `Stimulus` table.
stimulus_id.title: Stimulus Id

symbol_count.description: The number of symbols represented in this component.
symbol_count.title: Symbol Count

symbol_layout.description: How the symbols are laid out.
symbol_layout.title: Symbol Layout

symbol_name.description: The human-readable name of the displayed symbol.
symbol_name.title: Symbol Name

x_screen.description: X coordinates of the stimulus component relative to the left edge of the screen in pixels.
x_screen.title: X Screen

x_viewport.description: X coordinates of the stimulus component relative to the left edge of the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen width.
x_viewport.title: X Viewport

y_screen.description: Y coordinates of the stimulus component relative to the top edge of the screen in pixels.
y_screen.title: Y Screen

y_viewport.description: Y coordinates of the stimulus component relative to the top edge of the screen expressed as a fraction of the screen height.
y_viewport.title: Y Viewport

StimulusComponent.title: StimulusComponent

TrialData.description: Root container mapping each trial table to an array of its rows.
TrialData.title: TrialData

description: Root container mapping each trial table to an array of its rows.

@type.title: Type
@type.description: JSON-LD node type (rdf:type) for schema.org / Google Dataset Search discoverability.

title: trial
